summary: Agent Teams AI (777genius/agent-teams-ai) is a free Electron desktop app that orchestrates persistent teams of AI coding agents (a "lead" plus teammates) over Claude Code-style CLI runtimes, visualized as a real-time kanban board. Its core trick: it does NOT puppet multiple vendor CLIs directly — it ships a bundled fork of Claude Code called `claude-multimodel` (the "agent_teams_orchestrator" flavor, pinned in runtime.lock.json from the private repo 777genius/agent_teams_orchestrator) that natively supports `--provider anthropic|codex|gemini|opencode` per teammate, and it drives that fork via Claude Code's experimental agent-teams machinery (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1, stream-json stdin/stdout, tmux or headless teammate processes). State (teams, tasks, inboxes, kanban) lives as plain JSON under ~/.claude, the app file-watches it, and agents mutate it through a bundled MCP server backed by a shared `agent-teams-controller` JS package. It includes a full agent-to-agent review loop (review_request → review_start → review_approve / review_request_changes) plus a human hunk-level diff review UI.
tech_stack: TypeScript throughout. Electron 40 (electron-vite) with three layers: src/main (Node main process services), src/preload, src/renderer (React 19 + Zustand 4 + Tailwind). Vitest for tests, pnpm workspaces. Two embedded Node packages: `mcp-server/` (FastMCP 3.x MCP server exposing ~40 team/task/review tools) and `agent-teams-controller/` (plain CommonJS domain library that reads/writes the ~/.claude JSON state; shared by MCP server and app). Persistence is purely JSON files under ~/.claude (no DB): teams/<team>/config.json, teams/<team>/kanban-state.json, teams/<team>/inboxes/<member>.json, tasks/<team>/<taskId>.json, plus session JSONL transcripts under ~/.claude/projects. ssh2 + SFTP for read-only remote monitoring; a non-Electron entry (src/main/standalone.ts) serves the same HTTP API + static web UI for Docker/VPS. Heavy work (task-change diff computation, team FS scans) is offloaded to worker threads (src/main/workers/).
architecture: Electron main process hosts ~100 services under src/main/services/team/. The God-object is src/main/services/team/TeamProvisioningService.ts (~40k lines): it resolves the CLI binary (src/main/services/team/ClaudeBinaryResolver.ts — bundled resources/runtime/claude-multimodel first, or CLAUDE_AGENT_TEAMS_ORCHESTRATOR_CLI_PATH / CLAUDE_CLI_PATH / PATH; flavor chosen in src/main/services/team/cliFlavor.ts, default 'agent_teams_orchestrator'), builds env (buildProvisioningEnv sets CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS:'1', CLAUDE_TEAM_CONTROL_URL, provider creds via src/main/services/runtime/providerAwareCliEnv.ts which also sets CODEX_CLI_PATH to the app-managed @openai/codex binary), writes a deterministic bootstrap spec JSON (src/main/services/team/provisioning/TeamProvisioningBootstrapSpec.ts — version/runId/team/lead/members[] each with name, prompt, cwd, provider, model, effort, isolation:'worktree', mcpConfigPath), then spawns ONE long-lived lead CLI process. The CLI fork itself performs "deterministic team bootstrap": it creates the team config and spawns each teammate (tmux pane or detached headless process per member), emitting custom stream-json events ({"type":"system","subtype":"team_bootstrap","event":"member_spawn_started"/"member_spawn_result"/"phase_changed",...}) that TeamProvisioningService.handleDeterministicBootstrapEvent() consumes to drive per-member spawn status. Agents coordinate through the filesystem + the bundled MCP server (mcp-server/src/tools/*: task_create, task_start, task_complete, task_set_owner, message_send, review_request/start/approve/request_changes, kanban_*, cross_team_send, member_briefing, member_work_sync_report...), which calls into agent-teams-controller (agent-teams-controller/src/internal/{tasks,kanban,review,messages,messageStore}.js) to mutate ~/.claude JSON under file locks. The app's FileWatcher (src/main/services/infrastructure/FileWatcher.ts) watches ~/.claude/teams and ~/.claude/tasks (fs.watch + polling fallback) and pushes team-change events to the renderer kanban. An MCP HTTP stream server (src/main/services/team/AgentTeamsMcpHttpServer.ts, 127.0.0.1:43100+/mcp) serves OpenCode-style runtimes; stdio MCP configs are written per launch by src/main/services/team/TeamMcpConfigBuilder.ts (`--mcp-config <tmpfile>`). Renderer kanban: src/renderer/components/team/kanban/KanbanBoard.tsx with columns ['todo','in_progress','review','done','approved'].
claude_code_integration: All execution goes through the Claude Code CLI surface (vanilla `claude` or the bundled `claude-multimodel` fork). (1) Team lead launch (TeamProvisioningService.ts ~line 21446): spawnCli(claudePath, spawnArgs) where spawnArgs = ['--print','--input-format','stream-json','--output-format','stream-json','--verbose','--setting-sources','user,project,local','--mcp-config',mcpConfigPath,'--team-bootstrap-spec',bootstrapSpecPath, ('--team-bootstrap-user-prompt-file',promptPath), '--disallowedTools','TeamDelete,TodoWrite,TaskCreate,TaskUpdate,mcp__agent-teams__team_launch,mcp__agent-teams__team_stop', skipPermissions ? '--dangerously-skip-permissions','--permission-mode','bypassPermissions' : '--permission-prompt-tool','stdio','--permission-mode','default', '--model',launchModelArg, '--effort',effort, '--worktree',name, plus teammate-mode args]. Env includes CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS='1' and CLAUDE_ENABLE_DETERMINISTIC_TEAM_BOOTSTRAP='1'. stdio is ['pipe','pipe','pipe'] — pure subprocess, no PTY for the lead. (2) User→lead messages and post-compact reminders are injected as stream-json stdin lines: JSON.stringify({type:'user',message:{role:'user',content:[{type:'text',text}]}}) + '\n' written to child.stdin (~line 34602). Permission prompts arrive as stream-json control_request messages (can_use_tool → surfaced to UI; other subtypes auto-allowed). (3) Teammates: in tmux mode the fork creates panes; for direct restarts the app builds `(cd <cwd> && env K=V... '<claudePath>' --agent-id '<member>@<team>' --agent-name ... --team-name ... --agent-color ... --parent-session-id ... --setting-sources ... --mcp-config ... --disallowedTools ... --dangerously-skip-permissions --permission-mode bypassPermissions --model M --effort E); printf '__CLAUDE_TEAMMATE_EXIT__:%s'` and delivers it via tmux send-keys (buildDirectTmuxRestartCommand in src/main/services/team/provisioning/TeamProvisioningDirectRestart.ts). Headless restarts spawn the same binary with extra '--teammate-runtime','headless' flag, detached:true, env TEAMMATE_RUNTIME=headless and a TEAMMATE_RUNTIME_EVENTS file the app appends 'exited' events to (~line 15827-15899). (4) One-shot scheduled tasks (src/main/services/schedule/ScheduledTaskExecutor.ts): `claude -p <prompt> --output-format stream-json --verbose --max-turns N --no-session-persistence [--max-budget-usd X] [--model M] [--effort E] [--dangerously-skip-permissions] [--allowed-tools a,b] [--disallowed-tools ...]`. (5) Session resume/auto-resume: AutoResumeService parses rate-limit reset times from output and re-nudges the lead ("Your rate limit has reset...") via stdin; session_id is captured from stream-json (result.session_id) with a fallback scan of ~/.claude/projects for the newest JSONL. (6) Stop hooks: member-work-sync injects a Claude settings fragment {hooks:{Stop:[{matcher:'',hooks:[{type:'command',command:"/bin/sh '<script>' '<spoolRoot>' 'claude' '262144' # agent-teams:member-work-sync-turn-settled:v1"}]}]}} via app-managed --settings JSON (src/features/member-work-sync/main/infrastructure/runtimeTurnSettledHookSettings.ts) so every settled turn writes an event into a spool dir the app drains.
codex_integration: Codex is a provider INSIDE the bundled claude-multimodel fork, not a separately-orchestrated CLI (docs/claude-multimodel-integration-plan.md: "there is no separate Codex binary to install... 'codex' maps to the OpenAI/Codex path inside claude-multimodel"; the codex-native backend description in src/features/codex-account/renderer/mergeCodexProviderStatusWithSnapshot.ts is literally "Use codex exec JSON mode." / 'codex exec --json' — i.e., the fork shells out to the real codex CLI in exec JSON mode). The app: (a) installs/manages a real @openai/codex binary (src/features/codex-runtime-installer/main/infrastructure/CodexRuntimeInstallerService.ts resolves platform packages like '@openai/codex-darwin-arm64') and exports its path as env CODEX_CLI_PATH into every launch (src/main/services/runtime/providerAwareCliEnv.ts); (b) talks to `codex app-server` directly over JSON-RPC stdio for account/auth/rate-limits/model catalog: spawnCli(binaryPath, ['app-server']) in src/main/services/infrastructure/codexAppServer/CodexAppServerSessionFactory.ts + JsonRpcStdioClient.ts, calling account/read, account/rateLimits/read, account/logout, model/list, config/read (src/features/codex-account, src/features/codex-model-catalog); ChatGPT browser login is brokered through app-server (CodexLoginSessionManager.ts); (c) queries the fork for codex status/models: execCli(claudePath, ['model','list','--json','--provider','codex']) and ['runtime','status','--json','--provider','codex'] (TeamProvisioningService.readRuntimeProviderLaunchFacts ~line 4685, ClaudeMultimodelBridgeService.ts: ['auth','status','--json','--provider',providerId]); (d) launches codex teammates by setting provider:'codex' + model + effort in the bootstrap spec member entry — the same --model/--effort flags are passed to the fork, which routes to codex. Codex turn completion has no Stop hook, so the app passes env AGENT_TEAMS_RUNTIME_TURN_SETTLED_SPOOL_ROOT (runtimeTurnSettledEnvironment.ts) and the fork writes codex turn-settled events + full native traces under ~/.claude/teams/<team>/.member-work-sync/runtime-hooks/codex-native-traces/, read back by src/main/services/team/taskLogs/stream/CodexNativeTraceReader.ts (events with tool_start/tool_result/message projections) and normalized by CodexNativeTurnSettledPayloadNormalizer.ts.
status_detection: Multi-layered, mostly filesystem-first: (1) Lead stdout stream-json parsing — attachStdoutHandler (TeamProvisioningService.ts ~20793) splits NDJSON lines, handles types user/assistant/system/control_request/result, captures session_id, detects auth/API errors in raw text, tracks lead activity (active/idle) from message flow, and consumes custom team_bootstrap events for member spawn progress. A carry buffer handles JSON fragments split across chunks. (2) Filesystem monitor during provisioning (startFilesystemMonitor, FS_MONITOR_POLL_MS=2000) polls team config/members/tasks files instead of trusting stdout, and kills the lead early once config+members+tasks exist. (3) Stall watchdogs: startStallWatchdog (STALL_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS=10s / warning at 20s without stdout) for provisioning; src/main/services/team/stallMonitor/TeamTaskStallMonitor.ts periodically scans open workIntervals on in_progress tasks against task-log freshness (TeamTaskLogFreshnessReader) and notifies/nudges on stalls. (4) Turn-settled events: Claude teammates report via the injected Stop hook spool; codex/opencode via the spool-root env written by the fork; drained by RuntimeTurnSettledDrainScheduler + FileRuntimeTurnSettledEventStore into MemberWorkSyncReconciler, which recomputes each member's ActionableWorkAgenda and decides nudges. (5) Transcript tailing: TeamMemberLogsFinder + taskLogs/stream services tail ~/.claude/projects/<proj>/<sessionId>.jsonl and agent-*.jsonl files; TaskBoundaryParser locates task start/complete boundaries (TaskUpdate tool or MCP task tools) inside transcripts and attributes Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tool_use ids to tasks. (6) Headless teammate liveness: pid tracking + an events file appended on child 'close' ('exited' event), TeamRuntimeLivenessResolver, plus `ps eww -p <pid>` checks. (7) Kanban realtime: FileWatcher on ~/.claude/teams and ~/.claude/tasks with 300ms coalescing → IPC → renderer store. Exit codes from tmux teammates are recovered from the printed __CLAUDE_TEAMMATE_EXIT__:<code> sentinel.
task_and_board_model: Tasks are individual JSON files at ~/.claude/tasks/<teamName>/<taskId>.json (Claude Code CLI Zod-schema-compatible: description/blocks/blockedBy required — see TeamTaskWriter comment), written with per-path async locks + atomic temp-file writes. TeamTask (src/shared/types/team.ts): id, displayId, subject, description, prompt, owner, createdBy, status ('pending'|'in_progress'|'completed'|'deleted'), workIntervals[{startedAt,completedAt}], reviewIntervals[{reviewer,startedAt,completedAt}], historyEvents (append-only event log: task_created, status_changed, owner_changed, review_requested, review_started, review_changes_requested, review_approved), blocks/blockedBy (dependency graph — blocked tasks wait automatically), related, comments (typed: regular|review_request|review_approved), needsClarification ('lead'|'user'), attachments, sourceMessage. Review state ('none'|'review'|'needsFix'|'approved') is DERIVED from historyEvents, with a kanban overlay at ~/.claude/teams/<team>/kanban-state.json holding {tasks:{<id>:{column:'review'|'approved', reviewer, reviewStatus}}, reviewers:[...]} (TeamKanbanManager.ts / agent-teams-controller kanbanStore.js). UI columns todo/in_progress/review/done/approved are computed: explicit kanban column wins, else status maps (completed→done). Team config at ~/.claude/teams/<team>/config.json (members with name/role/agentType/color/providerId/model/effort), inboxes at teams/<team>/inboxes/<member>.json, sent messages, processes.json for registered live processes. All mutations route through agent-teams-controller under a team board lock (boardLock.js) whether invoked from the app UI (IPC) or by agents (MCP tools).
review_loop: Yes — first-class implementer→reviewer loop between agents. MCP tools (mcp-server/src/tools/reviewTools.ts) backed by agent-teams-controller/src/internal/review.js: review_request (task must be status 'completed'; sets kanban column 'review', appends review_requested with reviewer — defaults to kanban-state.reviewers[0] — and sends an inbox message to the reviewer), review_start (appends review_started, opens a reviewIntervals entry, enforces only the assigned reviewer or lead may review), review_approve (appends review_approved, moves kanban column to 'approved', optional notify-owner message), review_request_changes (closes review intervals, appends review_changes_requested→'needsFix', clears kanban with nextReviewState 'needsFix', sets task status back to 'pending', adds a review_request comment, then messages.sendMessage to the task OWNER: "@<reviewer> **requested changes** for task #X ... The task has been moved back to pending. When you are ready to resume, review the task context, start it explicitly, implement the fixes, mark it completed, and request review again."). Feedback routing is therefore: reviewer note → task comment + owner inbox message; the owner's next turn (or a work-sync nudge) picks it up; needsFix tasks count as actionable in ActionableWorkAgenda. The member-work-sync reconciler additionally enforces "review obligations": currentReviewCycle.ts derives review_pickup_required / review_in_progress per reviewer from history events, and MemberWorkSyncNudgeDispatcher inserts deduplicated review-pickup nudge messages directly into the reviewer's inbox (insertIfAbsent) when a settled-turn snapshot shows an unclaimed review. On top of the agent loop there is a human review layer: ChangeExtractorService + TaskChangeComputer build per-task diffs from transcript tool_use boundaries (with git-diff fallback, GitDiffFallback.ts), and ReviewApplierService.ts applies human decisions at hunk level (rejectHunks via snippet-level reverse replacement with inverse-patch fallback, file restore, conflict detection vs current disk content), with decisions persisted by ReviewDecisionStore.ts.
worktree_and_git: Per-teammate opt-in isolation: a member spec can carry isolation:'worktree'. TeamMemberWorktreeManager.ensureMemberWorktree() resolves the repo root (git rev-parse --show-toplevel), creates branch `agent-teams/<teamSlug>/<memberSlug>-<repoHash>` and worktree at <appData>/team-worktrees/<projectSlug>-<repoHash>/<teamSlug>/<memberSlug> via `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> HEAD` (reattaching with `git worktree add <path> <branch>` if the branch exists and isn't checked out elsewhere; validates existing worktrees match repo+branch via rev-parse --git-common-dir / --abbrev-ref HEAD). The teammate's cwd is then the worktree. The lead can also be launched with Claude Code's own `--worktree <name>` flag (request.worktree in spawnArgs). TeamWorktreeGitService.ts wraps git execFile calls; BranchStatusService.ts reports branch state to the UI; ChangeExtractorService/GitDiffFallback compute per-task diffs. There is NO automated PR creation, merging, or gh integration — integration of approved work is left to the user (the app's review UI applies/reverts hunks in the working tree instead). README notes branch rules can be spelled out in the provisioning prompt for agents to follow themselves.
other_agents: Four provider ids (src/main/services/team/runtime/TeamRuntimeAdapter.ts): 'anthropic', 'codex', 'gemini', 'opencode'. Abstraction is two-tiered: (1) most providers (anthropic/codex/gemini) are backends INSIDE the claude-multimodel fork, selected per member via the bootstrap spec's provider/model/effort fields and queried uniformly with `claude-multimodel {auth status|model list|runtime status} --json --provider <id>` (ClaudeMultimodelBridgeService.ts normalizes capabilities, model catalogs, reasoning-effort support, rate-limit snapshots per provider); (2) OpenCode is a genuinely separate runtime with its own adapter implementing TeamLaunchRuntimeAdapter (src/main/services/team/runtime/OpenCodeTeamRuntimeAdapter.ts + ~40 files under src/main/services/team/opencode/: bridge, delivery watchdogs, permission approval provider, readiness probes), connecting through the fork's opencode bridge plus the app's HTTP-stream MCP server (AgentTeamsMcpHttpServer on 127.0.0.1:43100+). Gemini has its own auth resolution (geminiRuntimeAuth.ts) and a post-launch hydration prompt injected via stdin because it loses bootstrap context. A RuntimeToolApprovalCoordinator unifies pending tool approvals across opencode/codex (TeamRuntimePendingApproval with requestId/decision allow|reject), while Claude approvals ride the stream-json control_request channel. Mixed teams are explicitly supported: each member resolves its own provider, model and effort (materializeEffectiveTeamMemberSpecs), with provider-aware default models (no global default).
remote_cloud: No cloud agent execution. Closest things: (1) src/main/standalone.ts — a pure-Node headless server (HOST/PORT/CLAUDE_ROOT env, default 0.0.0.0:3456) serving the renderer as static files + the same HTTP API, for Docker/VPS self-hosting (docker/ folder exists); agents still run on that machine. (2) Read-only SSH monitoring: SshConnectionManager + SshFileSystemProvider (ssh2/SFTP) let the UI browse a remote machine's ~/.claude data. (3) Roadmap (README, unchecked): "Remote agent execution via SSH: launch and manage agent teams on remote machines over SSH (stream-json protocol over SSH channel, SFTP-based file monitoring for tasks/inboxes/config)".
key_files: src/main/services/team/TeamProvisioningService.ts — 40k-line core: builds spawn args, spawns lead CLI, parses stream-json, manages member spawn/restart, stdin injection, watchdogs
src/main/services/team/ClaudeBinaryResolver.ts — resolves bundled claude-multimodel vs vanilla claude binary (flavors, PATH/nvm scan, cache)
src/main/services/team/provisioning/TeamProvisioningBootstrapSpec.ts — deterministic bootstrap spec JSON (per-member provider/model/effort/isolation/prompt) passed via --team-bootstrap-spec
src/main/services/team/provisioning/TeamProvisioningDirectRestart.ts — tmux send-keys command builder + env allowlist for teammate restarts
src/main/services/team/provisioning/TeamProvisioningPromptBuilders.ts — member spawn prompts (member_briefing-first bootstrap protocol)
src/main/services/runtime/ClaudeMultimodelBridgeService.ts — `claude-multimodel auth status/model list/runtime status --json --provider X` capability bridge
src/main/services/runtime/providerAwareCliEnv.ts — per-provider env (CODEX_CLI_PATH, OPENAI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN allowlists)
src/main/services/infrastructure/codexAppServer/CodexAppServerSessionFactory.ts — spawns `codex app-server` JSON-RPC for account/models/login
src/features/member-work-sync/main/infrastructure/runtimeTurnSettledHookSettings.ts — injected Claude Stop hook writing turn-settled spool events
src/features/member-work-sync/core/application/MemberWorkSyncReconciler.ts + core/domain/ActionableWorkAgenda.ts — agenda computation + idle-member nudging incl. review obligations
src/main/services/team/taskLogs/stream/CodexNativeTraceReader.ts — reads codex native trace files for task logs/status
agent-teams-controller/src/internal/review.js — review_request/start/approve/request_changes state machine + owner notification
agent-teams-controller/src/internal/runtimeHelpers.js — canonical ~/.claude paths (teams/, tasks/, kanban-state.json, inboxes/)
mcp-server/src/tools/ — FastMCP tool surface agents use (taskTools, reviewTools, messageTools, kanbanTools, workSyncTools, crossTeamTools)
src/main/services/team/TeamTaskWriter.ts / TeamTaskReader.ts — atomic per-task JSON persistence, CLI-schema compatible
src/main/services/team/TeamKanbanManager.ts — kanban-state.json overlay (review/approved columns, reviewers)
src/main/services/team/TeamMemberWorktreeManager.ts — git worktree per member (agent-teams/<team>/<member>-<hash> branches)
src/main/services/team/ReviewApplierService.ts + ChangeExtractorService.ts — hunk-level human review apply/reject over transcript-derived diffs
src/main/services/schedule/ScheduledTaskExecutor.ts — one-shot `claude -p ... --output-format stream-json` runner
src/main/services/infrastructure/FileWatcher.ts — watches ~/.claude/teams + ~/.claude/tasks to drive the realtime kanban
takeaways: Patterns worth stealing for a kanban app orchestrating Claude Code + Codex with per-column model defaults and an implement→review loop: (1) Filesystem as the coordination bus, not stdout: persist tasks as one JSON file each with an append-only historyEvents log (task_created/status_changed/review_requested/review_changes_requested/review_approved) and DERIVE review state + kanban column from events; watch the directory (fs.watch + polling fallback, 300ms coalesce) to drive the board. This survives crashes, restarts, and multiple writers, and makes 'needsFix' a derived column rather than a status. (2) Don't trust a single signal for agent status — combine: stream-json stdout parsing for the foreground process, a Stop-hook spool for Claude turn completion (inject hooks via inline --settings JSON, write events to a spool dir, drain asynchronously), an env-pointed spool for Codex (no hook system — have your wrapper/notify write turn-settled events), exit-code sentinels for shell-hosted processes, and a stall monitor comparing open workIntervals against log freshness. (3) Claude Code headless drive: `claude --print --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --verbose --mcp-config <file> --permission-mode bypassPermissions|default --permission-prompt-tool stdio --model M --effort E`; send user messages as `{type:'user',message:{role:'user',content:[...]}}` stdin lines; answer control_request for interactive approvals; capture result.session_id with a newest-JSONL fallback. Codex management plane: `codex app-server` JSON-RPC stdio for auth/model list/rate limits, `codex exec --json` for execution. (4) Per-member (≈per-column) model defaults: carry {providerId, model, effort} on each member/task spec, resolve provider-aware defaults at launch (`model list --json --provider X`), never a single global default model. (5) Review loop mechanics: gate review_request on status==='completed'; assign a reviewer from a reviewers list; on request_changes, atomically (comment + status→pending + kanban clear + inbox message to the owner with explicit resume instructions), and run a reconciler that nudges the reviewer when a review sits unclaimed (dedup by agenda fingerprint so nudges aren't spammed). (6) Give agents an MCP toolbelt over your board (task_create/start/complete, review_*, message_send) backed by the same locked store your UI uses — one source of truth, board locks (file locks) around multi-file transitions, atomic temp-file writes. (7) Worktree isolation: branch naming `agent-teams/<team>/<member>-<repoHash>` under an app-owned worktrees dir, validate existing worktrees (git-common-dir + branch) before reuse. (8) Cautionary tale: their per-teammate flags (--agent-id/--team-name/--team-bootstrap-spec) only work because they forked Claude Code; with stock CLIs you instead get the same effect by spawning each teammate yourself as an independent `claude -p`/`codex exec` run keyed to a task file, which is also simpler to status-track. Also note their scheduled one-shot runner uses --max-turns/--max-budget-usd/--no-session-persistence — good hygiene flags for queue-style task execution.
